etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, "wood") + 不(不) (bù, "not") = semantic wood + phonetic element -> final form
etymology.interpretation: The character 杯(bēi, "cup") uses 木(木) (mù, "wood") as the semantic component, indicating a wooden object, and 不(不) (bù, "not") as the phonetic component to suggest pronunciation. The inclusion of wood likely references the historical use of wood in making drinking vessels.

examples.chinese: 我有一个新杯子(我有一個新杯子)。
他给我倒了一杯茶(他給我倒了一杯茶)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gěi wǒ dào le yī bēi chá.
examples.english: He poured me a cup of tea.

examples.chinese: 请用这个杯子喝水(請用這個杯子喝水)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng yòng zhège bēizi hē shuǐ.
examples.english: Please use this cup to drink water.
